channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ex-ballerina convicted of killing husband in 'Black Swan' case gets 20 years in prison
item.description: Ashley Benefield, who was found guilty of manslaughter for shooting her husband during a domestic argument in 2020 sentencing hearing is set for today.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing Missouri mother found dead, boyfriend charged with murder: police
item.description: Authorities in Missouri announced that a man is behind bars for the murder of his girlfriend, whose body was found in a wooded area, after he had reported her missing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: E. coli outbreak linked to McDonald's onions is over: CDC
item.description: An outbreak across 14 states in October connected to McDonald’s slivered onions from the popular menu item impacted at least 100 people.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missouri executes a man for sexually assaulting and strangling a 9-year-old girl in 2007
item.description: BONNE TERRE, Mo. (AP) — Rowan Ford knew Christopher Collings as “Uncle Chris” after he spent several months living with her family. On Tuesday, Collings was executed for sexually assaulting and killing the child, then dumping her body in a sinkhole outside a small Missouri town.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: App claims its AI can predict when you'll die
item.description: The app also features a countdown clock that features your death day and a picture of the Grim Reaper.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: US border arrests drop 17% in November, dousing predictions of post-election surge
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — U.S. authorities made about 46,700 arrests for illegally crossing the border from Mexico in November, down about 17% from October to a new low for Joe Biden's presidency, an official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Freedom is permanent for Missourian described as the longest-held wrongly incarcerated woman in US
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Years of legal wrangling have come to an end for a woman who spent 43 years behind bars for a killing that her attorneys argue was committed by a discredited police officer.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What happened to Hannah Kobayashi? Police say the Hawaii woman voluntarily disappeared
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old woman from Hawaii who was reported missing in Los Angeles three weeks ago, walked freely into Mexico, police said. Authorities declared Kobayashi a “voluntary missing person,” meaning she left of her own free will.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California cuts back on water deliveries, anticipating dry year
item.description: The State Water Project is planning for a dry 2025 punctuated by extreme storms like we’ve seen in late November,” Karla Nemeth said,
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Jon Stewart mocks Dems, Fox News on Hunter Biden pardon
item.description: “The Democrats made this case an example of why Americans should believe in our system," Comedian Jon Stewart said.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: FBI offers $15K reward in case of Maine teen missing since September
item.description: The teenager reportedly had an argument with her sister before walking out of her house into the woods.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck workers told to stay home from work for next 3 days: report
item.description: (KRON) -- Production line workers at the Tesla Cybertruck factory in Austin were told to take the next three days, according to a report in Business Insider.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez first person to hit a million followers on Bluesky
item.description: The platform’s features are based off of Twitter, and they include the ability to message fellow users as well as a discover and feed tab.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police say a Hawaii woman disappeared voluntarily and traveled to Mexico
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Hawaii woman who vanished after landing in Los Angeles three weeks ago disappeared voluntarily as she sought to “step away from modern connectivity” and was last seen crossing into Mexico with her luggage, police said at a news conference where they urged her to contact her dist
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's the top 25 list of most-viewed Wikipedia articles of 2024
item.description: We collectively spent an estimated 2.4 billion hours reading English-language articles on Wikipedia in 2024, and we now know which were the most popular.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Adams, KRON4

item.title: Zach Bryan fan strikes lottery gold during marathon concert road trip
item.description: A whirlwind road trip to see country music star Zach Bryan in concert ended on an even higher note for a California mother and her teen son, thanks to a winning lottery ticket.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: These are the worst cities for package theft in 2024
item.description: The holiday season, which has already seen a surge in online shopping, presents even more opportunities for package thieves.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KTLA 5

item.title: LAPD give update on Maui woman who vanished in Los Angeles
item.description: Authorities are set to update the public Monday evening with the latest details on Hannah Kobayashi, a 30-year-old Maui resident who has been the center of a massive search since she vanished in Los Angeles in early November.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Wave of Democrats slam Biden's pardon of his son Hunter
item.description: While some Democrats extended a measure of sympathy, President Biden has taken heat on several fronts.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prosecution makes closing argument in murder trial over stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Closing arguments began on Monday in the trial of a tech consultant in the 2023 stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee in San Francisco, an event that shocked the broader tech community whose members mourned the loss of an entrepreneur they called charismatic and kind.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California bill would give public university admission priority to slaves' descendants
item.description: California lawmakers are set to consider a bill that would require the state's public universities to give admission priority to the descendants of slaves.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, TRÂN NGUYỄN and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Unions score a major win in Wisconsin with a court ruling restoring collective bargaining rights
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin public worker and teachers unions scored a major legal victory Monday with a ruling that restores collective bargaining rights they lost under a 2011 state law that sparked weeks of protests and made the state the center of the national battle over union rights.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Abnormal brain and childhood abuse make Missouri man unfit for execution, his lawyers say
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — A Missouri man facing the death penalty for sexually assaulting and killing a child was a frequent victim of physical and sexual abuse in his youth and has a “structurally abnormal” brain that impairs his judgment, according to a clemency petition filed on his behalf.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hegseth forced out of vets groups due to drinking, misconduct: New Yorker
item.description: The incidents — relayed in a trail of documents and corroborated to The New Yorker by the accounts of former colleagues — took place prior to Hegseth becoming a full-time Fox News TV host in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: How long do Thanksgiving leftovers last? Here's what you need to know
item.description: Refrigerators nationwide are likely filled with Thanksgiving leftovers, but according to FoodSafety.gov, today is the last day most leftovers will be safe to eat. The federal government's online food safety resource says that Thanksgiving leftovers can only survive in the fridge for four days.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Jamie Komoroski sentenced for Folly Beach DUI crash that killed new bride
item.description: CHARLESTON, S.C. (WCBD) – The woman accused of drunkenly crashing into a golf cart, killing a bride, and seriously injuring members of the wedding party on Folly Beach last year was sentenced during a Monday afternoon hearing in Charleston for charges related to the incident.
item.dc:creator: Jameson Moyer, Fox 5

item.title: White House unveils 'A Season of Peace and Light' Christmas decorations
item.description: First Lady Jill Biden has decked out the White House with some whimsical decorations to evoke the “peace and light” of the season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Intel CEO steps down as chipmaker falters, lays off staff
item.description: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has stepped down after a three-year stint leading the company, the chipmaker announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Chainsaw-wielding man killed by police at assisted living facility in Illinois
item.description: The man had gotten into the lobby of the building and started confronting residents with the chainsaw, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KRON4

item.title: Hannah Kobayashi's missed flight was 'intentional': LAPD chief
item.description: Kobayashi disappeared and stopped contacting her family just days after missing a connecting flight at LAX.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Christian McCaffrey's season 'potentially' over after knee injury in 49ers' loss to Bills
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — San Francisco running back Christian McCaffrey suffered a knee injury that coach Kyle Shanahan described as “potentially” season-ending in the 49ers’ 35-10 loss to the Buffalo Bills on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US Coast Guard is searching for 5 people after a fishing boat reportedly capsized in seas off Alaska
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard said Sunday it's searching for five people after a fishing boat reportedly capsized in rough weather and seas near Alaska's capital of Juneau.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Girl Scout saves dad days after learning CPR with her troop
item.description: Just days before Vada Carawan's father suffered a heart attack, the 11-year-old and her Girl Scout troop had learned CPR.
item.dc:creator: Deja Parker, KTLA 5

item.title: Big Ten fines Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for postgame melee
item.description: ROSEMONT, Ill. (AP) — The Big Ten Conference announced it fined Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for violating the conference's sportsmanship policy for the on-field melee at the end of the Wolverines' win in Columbus on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Heavy snow and numbing temperatures keep parts of the US in a deep freeze
item.description: Heavy snowfall and numbing temperatures kept parts of the U.S. in a deep freeze Sunday as the Thanksgiving holiday weekend draws to a close.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5
